summary: Stancil argues that the modern data stack, once the corrective to Hadoop-era pain, has grown into an overgrown ecosystem of tools, costs, and complexity. He suggests we are entering a liminal period where AI/LLMs will trigger a new discontinuity, making the modern data stack’s philosophy ill-suited for the next era. The core claim is that data teams and vendors must adapt to AI-native infrastructures or risk becoming yesterday’s stack.
money_quotes: The punchline is the entire modern data stack.
We had 99 problems, so we used the modern data stack—and now we have 100 problems.
I now believe we’re in the liminal space between two eras.
Artificial intelligence, it seems, will be the next discontinuity.
For the modern data stack, this is it.
key_insight: The modern data stack’s tool-heavy philosophy is peaking and will likely be eclipsed by AI-driven “intelligent infrastructure” that reshapes how data systems and teams are built.
